title: Port stealth dynamic extension-frame filtering to legacy call attribution
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Source: #1154 review item R24 (datadir/review-1154.md:154-169).

## Problem (data hygiene, NOT detection)
Legacy's call attribution (`getOriginatingScriptContext`) uses a brittle fixed `trace[3]`
offset (`Extension/src/lib/js-instruments.ts:352`) and records `trace.slice(3)`. This assumes
extension frames appear only as a LEADING prefix. They don't: when a page calls back into an
instrumented API, MID-STACK extension frames slip through the fixed offset and pollute the
captured `call_stack`.

NOTE on observability: the captured `call_stack` goes to OpenWPM's DB, NOT to the page — this
is attribution QUALITY, not a detection vector. (The page-observable error `.stack` leak is a
separate item, R16.) The instrument's own wrapper frames are noise that degrades attribution
for every downstream analysis.

## Goal
Replace the fixed `trace[3]` offset with stealth's dynamic approach:
- Find the call site dynamically (first non-`getURL("")` frame), as stealth does.
- Use stealth's `filterExtensionFrames` to drop EVERY `moz-extension://` frame, including
  mid-stack ones, from the recorded `call_stack`.
- Result: robust attribution with no magic offset; the instrument's own frames never appear in
  the captured stack.

## Relationships
- Sibling to R16 and to GH #1204 (channel hardening); part of the legacy-footprint /
  attribution-quality hardening umbrella.
- `getOriginatingScriptContext` is where legacy and stealth genuinely diverge (stealth already
  strips moz-extension frames) — see datadir/stealth-helper-parity.md.
